metadata.description: Self-hosted best-practice plugins: UI/UX stacks, frameworks, databases, architecture, design patterns, testing, security, API design, dev environments, browser automation, and the taskmaster clarification-to-execution workflow suite.

plugins.name: ui-ux
plugins.description: UI/UX best practices with per-stack skills: shadcn/ui, ReUI, Aceternity UI, Astryx, Tailwind, CSS3, Bootstrap, CSS Grid, Flexbox — plus a design-tokens skill (spacing/type/radius/elevation/motion scales + semantic color tiering), theme creation for shadcn/ReUI/Aceternity, Tailwind, or Bootstrap with a live colour preview, a /ui-ux:build entry point, and ui-ux-reviewer + ui-ux-engineer agents.

plugins.name: react
plugins.description: React best practices: hooks rules, render/memo performance, state management, component patterns — plus a react-server-state skill for server-cache libraries (TanStack Query/SWR/RTK Query), cache keys and invalidation, mutations and optimistic updates, keeping server data out of useState and avoiding refetch storms.

plugins.name: react-native
plugins.description: React Native best practices: list performance, navigation, platform-specific code, animations.

plugins.name: vue2
plugins.description: Vue 2.7 best practices: Composition API backport, reactivity caveats, migration readiness. Vue 2 is EOL (Dec 2023) — for maintaining legacy apps only; new work should use vue3.

plugins.name: vue3
plugins.description: Vue 3 best practices: script setup, composables, ref/reactive pitfalls, Pinia.

plugins.name: nextjs
plugins.description: Next.js best practices: App Router server/client boundaries, opt-in caching (fetch, revalidate, use cache), server actions as public endpoints, route handlers, streaming with Suspense, metadata API, next/image and next/font — version-aware from 14 through 16.

plugins.name: nuxt
plugins.description: Nuxt best practices: Nitro server routes, hybrid rendering route rules, useFetch/useAsyncData payload dedup, useState SSR-safe state, auto-imports discipline, runtimeConfig env overrides, SEO meta.

plugins.name: php
plugins.description: PHP best practices: strict types, === discipline, PSR conventions, version-aware 8.1-8.5 leverage map pinned to the composer.json floor, exceptions, DateTimeImmutable, boundary security, static analysis.

plugins.name: laravel
plugins.description: Laravel best practices: Eloquent N+1 prevention, form requests, service layer, queues, policies, a per-version leverage map (Laravel 10/11/12/13), and mass-assignment security — plus the shared backend-engineer worker (PHP/Laravel) the php/laravel review commands route their fixes to.

plugins.name: livewire
plugins.description: Livewire 3/4 best practices: component conventions, wire:model modifiers, performance, Alpine interop.

plugins.name: sql
plugins.description: Engine-agnostic SQL best practices: sargable predicates, join correctness, indexing logic, NULL three-valued logic, transactions, constraints, keyset pagination, safe migrations, parameterization.

plugins.name: mysql
plugins.description: MySQL 8.0+ best practices: InnoDB clustered-PK design, utf8mb4, strict sql_mode, online DDL, gap locks, version leverage 8.0 through 8.4 and 9.7 LTS, EXPLAIN ANALYZE.

plugins.name: mariadb
plugins.description: MariaDB 10.6+ best practices: not-MySQL divergences, RETURNING, sequences, system-versioned tables, UUID type, uca1400 collations, JSON-as-text reality, Galera awareness.

plugins.name: postgresql
plugins.description: PostgreSQL 14+ best practices: MVCC and vacuum reality, rich types (timestamptz, jsonb, uuid), index arsenal (partial, expression, GIN, BRIN, CONCURRENTLY), lock-aware migrations, ON CONFLICT/RETURNING, version leverage through 17/18.

plugins.name: code-architecture
plugins.description: Engineering process: plan-before-code, YAGNI, SOLID principles applied with judgment, task orchestration, work verification, low-cognitive-load code, KISS/DRY simplicity principles. Owns code-level structure — units, interfaces, file placement; defers system-level topology (service boundaries, scaling, caching) to the system-design plugin.

plugins.name: design-patterns
plugins.description: Design pattern selection across the full GoF catalog: which pattern fits where, when NOT to use one, and look-alike disambiguation.

plugins.name: api-docs-first
plugins.description: Verify current API docs before writing integration code; ask for a URL or file when docs are missing. For consuming third-party APIs; defers designing your own REST APIs to api-design.

plugins.name: taskmaster
plugins.description: Idea-to-execution clarification: brainstorm fuzzy ideas into approved designs, grill requirements until zero ambiguity, deliberate the implementation approach with a blind four-persona round, decide visual choices with mockups, validate whole experiences with an interactive clickable demo, then emit milestone-grouped single-prompt task cards. Outputs live under taskmaster-docs/ (no collision with a project's own docs/).

plugins.name: design-preview
plugins.description: Real-component visual decisions for Vite + React projects: renders 2-3 candidate variants with the project's OWN components on its own dev server via a scratch HTML entry (zero edits to existing files), behind a strict consent gate with guaranteed cleanup. Escalation tier above static shell mockups — falls back to them when Vite is absent. Includes /design-preview:preview.

plugins.name: shadcn-studio
plugins.description: Greenfield interactive shadcn staging: stands up a self-contained shadcn + Vite (Tailwind v4) sandbox on its own dev server and renders agent-authored component variants side by side — real interactivity (sort/filter/dialog), not static HTML. For idea-stage / non-React work where design-preview cannot run (no host Vite+React app). Consent-gated, scratch outside the work tree, verified cleanup, static-shell fallback. Includes /shadcn-studio:stage.

plugins.name: git-workflow
plugins.description: Git workflow discipline: isolated worktrees for feature work, a structured finish protocol for development branches (verify, present options, merge/PR/park, clean up), and review-exchange rigor — what to include when requesting review, and technical verification before implementing received feedback. Includes /git-workflow:finish.

plugins.name: debugging
plugins.description: Systematic debugging: root cause before any fix — reproduce first, read the actual error, isolate with hypothesis tests and bisection, one change at a time, verify against the original symptom; escalation rule after three failed fixes. Ships a delegatable debugger agent for handing off the long reproduce-bisect investigation. Includes /debugging:debug.

plugins.name: task-runner
plugins.description: Disciplined task execution: one task at a time, scope locked to the task, bounded verify-fix inner loop per task, no drift, no unbounded outer loop. Completion means every check passed with evidence. Ships the shared task-executor agent — the delegatable 'apply the fixes' sink every review routes to — and a PostToolUse scope-lock tripwire that flags edits outside the current task's declared files. Includes parallel-planning: computed subagents-vs-inline verdict with dependency levels, agent count, and speedup estimate via /task-runner:plan.

plugins.name: stack-scan
plugins.description: Inventory the project's actual stack: manifests and lockfiles (composer, npm/yarn/pnpm/bun), runtimes, engine constraints, docker images. Reports required vs installed versions and flags drift, missing locks, and EOL majors.

plugins.name: plugin-scout
plugins.description: Scan the current project's manifests and suggest which cc-plugins-marketplace plugins to install — stack-matched tier and always-useful tier — then install the picked ones after confirmation. Includes /plugin-scout:suggest.

plugins.name: testing
plugins.description: Testing best practices: test pyramid and what to actually test, Pest/PHPUnit and Vitest/Jest idioms, Playwright/Dusk e2e discipline, factories and fixtures, mocking boundaries, flaky-test causes, coverage traps — plus the TDD workflow (red-green-refactor, red-green regression proof for bug fixes). Includes /testing:review and a test-engineer worker agent.

plugins.name: security
plugins.description: Security for web apps: OWASP-aligned code review (injection, XSS, CSRF, authz vs authn, mass assignment, uploads, secrets, dependency audit) mapped to PHP/Laravel and JS/Vue, PLUS design-phase threat-modeling (STRIDE, trust boundaries), a data-privacy (GDPR/CCPA) regulatory lens, and an api-auth lens (token model, OAuth2+PKCE, scopes). Includes /security:review and a security-engineer worker agent.

plugins.name: javascript
plugins.description: Vanilla JavaScript best practices: version-aware ES feature floors from engines/browserslist/lockfile, strict equality and coercion traps, ESM vs CommonJS interop, async correctness and the event loop, this-binding and closures, immutability, error handling, boundary validation, number precision and BigInt, security at boundaries. Includes /javascript:review.

plugins.name: typescript
plugins.description: TypeScript best practices: strict mode as the floor, any vs unknown discipline, narrowing over assertions, generics restraint, satisfies, runtime validation at boundaries, tsconfig hygiene. Includes /typescript:review.

plugins.name: node-backend
plugins.description: Server-side Node.js best practices for Express 5, NestJS 11, and Fastify 5: middleware vs DI vs plugin-encapsulation architecture, async error propagation, validation at the boundary (zod, class-validator, JSON Schema), streaming and backpressure, graceful shutdown, config/env discipline, per-framework footguns.

plugins.name: vite
plugins.description: Vite best practices: VITE_-prefix env security, dep pre-bundling, code splitting and manualChunks, base for sub-path deploys, dev server.proxy, define stringify pitfalls, import.meta.glob, asset handling, build.target alignment, SSR, library mode, plugin order, HMR guards, version-aware against the locked vite version and vite.config. Includes /vite:review.

plugins.name: inertia
plugins.description: Inertia.js v1/v2/v3 best practices (Laravel + Vue/React/Svelte adapters): partial reloads, deferred and lazy props, useForm flow, shared data via middleware, prop hygiene, SSR, code splitting, v1 vs v2 and adapter awareness. Includes /inertia:review.

plugins.name: api-design
plugins.description: REST API design: resource naming, status-code discipline, pagination/filtering/sorting conventions, versioning strategy, RFC 9457 error format, idempotency, Laravel API Resources, plus a graphql-grpc skill for non-REST surfaces (DataLoader, resolver authz, proto field-number safety, streaming). Includes /api-design:review and /api-design:scaffold (spec-first: OpenAPI → Laravel routes/FormRequests/Resources). Defers third-party API docs to api-docs-first.

plugins.name: dev-env
plugins.description: Dev environment initiator: scan composer/npm manifests, lockfiles, .env DSNs, and CI images, then generate a docker-compose.yml and Dockerfile matched to the actual stack — PHP version and extensions, Node, database engine, Redis, queues, mail. Includes /dev-env:init to generate and /dev-env:review to audit existing docker files. Pairs with stack-scan. Owns local development environments; defers CI/CD pipelines and production deploys to the devops plugin.

plugins.name: web-dev
plugins.description: Generalist web development pair: a web-developer worker (routing, REST/API integration, forms, state, SSR/CSR) and a frontend-reviewer that audits React/Vue/Inertia/Livewire/TS + react-native/vite against the matching per-framework skill. Stack-agnostic; defers stack idioms to the per-framework plugins.

plugins.name: system-design
plugins.description: System-level design: service boundaries on data ownership, scaling paths, cache placement, sync vs async integration and its failure modes, single points of failure, plus domain modeling (bounded contexts, aggregates, ubiquitous language) and an event-driven skill (delivery semantics, outbox, sagas, DLQ) — firing system-design, domain-modeling, and event-driven skills, a /system-design:review command, and a system-architect worker + system-design-reviewer pair. Complements code-architecture (code-level structure) without overlapping it.

plugins.name: devops
plugins.description: DevOps pipeline and infrastructure: CI/CD ordering, container/image hygiene, Kubernetes resource limits and probes, deploy strategy with rollback, and secrets handling — a firing devops-practices skill, a /devops:review command, and a devops-engineer worker + devops-reviewer pair. Owns infra-layer observability wiring (defers in-code instrumentation to observability); defers local docker-compose dev environments to dev-env.

plugins.name: database
plugins.description: Database design and implementation: engine-agnostic schema, migration (expand→contract), indexing, query-shape, and connection-pool discipline — a firing database-design skill, a /database:review command, a database-engineer worker, and a PreToolUse guard that asks for confirmation before a destructive statement (DROP/TRUNCATE/unqualified DELETE-UPDATE) lands. Defers dialect statement audits to the sql/mysql/mariadb/postgresql plugins.

plugins.name: performance
plugins.description: Performance tuning: measure-first profiling, N+1/index/payload/bundle/Core Web Vitals hotspots, cache correctness (stampede, TTL, eviction, staleness), and percentile load testing — a firing performance-tuning skill, a /performance:review command, and a worker agent that optimizes only what a measurement proves slow.

plugins.name: claude-authoring
plugins.description: Authoring guides for Claude Code artifacts — skills, agents, commands, hooks, plugins — plus a routine-detector that suggests scaffolding a project skill when work turns repetitive, a project-skill-suggester that proactively offers one when a task's cards share uncovered repository-specific knowledge, and /claude-authoring:new-skill|new-agent|new-command|new-hook|new-plugin scaffold commands.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Stack-agnostic code review: correctness bugs, code smells, convention drift on any diff or PR — severity-sorted one-line findings. Defers structure/YAGNI to code-architecture, security depth to security, stack idioms to the per-framework review plugins. Includes /code-review:review, a code-reviewer agent, and a code-smells skill.

plugins.name: approaches
plugins.description: Solution-approach deliberation: before non-trivial implementation, generate 2-3 structurally different approaches, compare trade-offs, pick with a stated kill-trigger — plus a strategy catalog (tracer bullet, walking skeleton, spike, strangler fig, inversion, Polya) mapped to the risk each one beats, and an auto-nudged opinion round where four parallel blind opinion-lens subagents (Standards Purist, Quality-over-Speed, Pragmatist-Minimalist, Skeptic-Investigator) argue refactor-shaped tasks and synthesis converges to one pick in a single round. Includes /approaches:compare and /approaches:opinions.

plugins.name: hindsight
plugins.description: Cross-session self-improvement loop: mines session transcripts for recurring friction and proposes CLAUDE.md rules, skill/plugin ideas, and failed-approach warnings — applied only on approval.

plugins.name: brain
plugins.description: A committed, Obsidian-style codebase map for a project: brain/INDEX.md indexes areas, key files, and notable classes/entrypoints so a fresh Claude session starts oriented. A SessionStart hook injects the compact map (with a staleness hint when it lags HEAD); /brain reads it and /brain index builds/refreshes it. Map-only tracer (Phase 1a); an anchored-notes layer is planned. Nothing outside brain/ is ever modified.

plugins.name: build-vs-buy
plugins.description: Gate zero before writing generic capability: check whether a library, service, or stdlib feature already solves it — candidate health table, take/wrap/write verdict, never-hand-roll list (crypto, auth, timezones, parsers). A UserPromptSubmit hook nudges the check when a prompt proposes building a commonly-solved capability. Includes /build-vs-buy:check.

plugins.name: rollout
plugins.description: Per-feature rollout planning: feature flags, backward compatibility, staged exposure (internal → percentage → full), migration sequencing (expand-migrate-contract), and a rollback path stated BEFORE ship. Includes /rollout:plan.

plugins.name: resilience
plugins.description: Failure-mode design for integration points: timeouts everywhere, retries with backoff and idempotency, circuit breaking, graceful degradation, backpressure, delivery semantics — designed in, not bolted on after the outage. Includes /resilience:review.

plugins.name: docs-upkeep
plugins.description: Documentation drift prevention: after any change, sync README claims, changelog entries, ADR links, and API docs with what the code now actually does — docs updated in the same change that invalidated them. Includes /docs-upkeep:check.

plugins.name: packages
plugins.description: Composer/npm dependency hygiene: semver constraint strategy, lockfile discipline, security-audit triage, and patch/minor/major upgrade lanes. Managing deps already in the project — whether to add one is build-vs-buy's job.

plugins.name: orchestration
plugins.description: Subagent orchestration discipline: delegation contracts (self-contained prompts, compressed evidence-backed returns, model/effort tiering, scout-then-fanout, writer isolation), verification panels (refuter voting, judge panels, loop-until-dry, completeness critic), and agent conventions (engineer/reviewer naming taxonomy, one-surface PROACTIVELY arbitration, the shared apply-fixes contract). Includes /orchestration:review.

plugins.name: estimation
plugins.description: Task sizing and effort estimation: S/M/L/XL classes with concrete anchors, reference-class calibration against work actually completed, uncertainty multipliers for unknowns, split triggers for oversized work, and estimate-vs-actual tracking persisted to a durable append-only ledger (with deferred-work parking) so calibration accumulates across runs. Includes /estimation:size.

plugins.name: a11y
plugins.description: Dedicated accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA audit for markup, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus management, forms, media, and ARIA — one line per violation with the concrete fix — plus an a11y-engineer worker that applies the fix list, preferring native semantics over ARIA patches and tagging each change with its WCAG criterion. Includes /a11y:audit.

plugins.name: observability
plugins.description: Application observability with judgment: structured JSON logs with correlation IDs, log levels that mean something, RED/USE metrics without cardinality bombs, trace-context propagation, symptom-based alerting, honest health checks — plus an observability-engineer worker that adds the instrumentation (deferring infra-layer wiring to devops). Includes /observability:review.

plugins.name: error-handling
plugins.description: Language-agnostic error-handling discipline: crash on programmer errors, handle operational errors where you can act, typed errors over message-string matching, cause chains preserved across boundaries, one report per failure — no swallowed exceptions, no catch-log-continue towers. Includes /error-handling:review.

plugins.name: concurrency
plugins.description: Application-level concurrency safety: check-then-act races, optimistic vs pessimistic locking, idempotency keys for retried operations, queue-consumer dedup under at-least-once delivery, distributed locks with TTL and fencing, async parallel-write pitfalls — safe when two copies run at once. Includes /concurrency:review.

plugins.name: playwright
plugins.description: Playwright automation navigator: resolves the current API from live docs (playwright.dev), link map per area (locators, auto-waiting, network interception, storageState auth, test runner, trace viewer, connectOverCDP), and robust-automation patterns. Includes /playwright:check and a Playwright-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.

plugins.name: puppeteer
plugins.description: Puppeteer automation navigator: current API from live docs (pptr.dev), link map (page API, waitForSelector, request interception, puppeteer.connect over CDP, puppeteer-extra stealth), and robust-automation patterns. Includes /puppeteer:check and a Puppeteer-keyword reminder hook. Defers general third-party doc verification to api-docs-first.

plugins.name: automation-builder
plugins.description: Browser-automation planner and worker: a think-process skill that turns an automation goal into a tool choice (Playwright vs Puppeteer) and a sequenced plan, plus a browser-automation-engineer agent that scaffolds and runs the automation. Includes /automation-builder:build and an automation-keyword reminder hook. Pairs with the playwright and puppeteer plugins; defers doc verification to api-docs-first.

plugins.name: frontend-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the frontend category in one install — UI/UX stacks, React, React Native, Vue 3, Next.js, Nuxt, JavaScript, TypeScript, Vite, Three.js, Inertia, Livewire, the generalist web worker, real-component visual decisions (design-preview), greenfield shadcn staging (shadcn-studio), and accessibility auditing. Uninstall cleanly with /frontend-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: php-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the PHP category in one install — PHP and Laravel best practices, Livewire, Inertia, Vite (Laravel's default asset bundler), and the generalist web worker. Uninstall cleanly with /php-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: db-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the database category in one install — engine-agnostic SQL discipline, MySQL, MariaDB, PostgreSQL best practices, and the database worker agent. Uninstall cleanly with /db-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: quality-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the code-quality category in one install — review, architecture principles, design patterns, testing, security, accessibility, debugging, performance, resilience, dependency hygiene, observability, error handling, concurrency safety, and secret-leak prevention (secret-scanning). Uninstall cleanly with /quality-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: process-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the engineering-process category in one install — git workflow, approach deliberation, hindsight mining, build-vs-buy gates, rollout planning, docs upkeep, estimation, subagent orchestration, task execution, stack scanning, plugin scouting, and intent-drift guarding (intent-guard). Uninstall cleanly with /process-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: everything
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: installs every plugin in this marketplace as a dependency — all stacks, all workflows, all agents. One install, full suite. Uninstall cleanly with /everything:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: taskmaster-suite
plugins.description: Meta-bundle: the full clarification-to-execution workflow plus its wired companions — taskmaster, task-runner, stack-scan, code-architecture, the stack-agnostic review/process toolkit, and the ui-ux agents the pipeline's visual cards route to. Uninstall cleanly with /taskmaster-suite:uninstall — removes the bundle and prunes its auto-installed plugins.

plugins.name: skill-router
plugins.description: File-aware skill auto-routing: a PostToolUse hook injects a directive to load the relevant best-practice skill(s) when you edit a matching file (PHP/Laravel/Blade, TS/JS, React/Vue/Inertia/Livewire, SQL, components, tests, Dockerfiles, OpenAPI), a SessionStart hook primes a repo-tailored skill index, and low-confidence content signals surface in a SessionEnd digest. Every relevant skill co-fires per edit (a11y alongside ui-ux); fail-open, once per skill per session.

plugins.name: secret-scanning
plugins.description: Secret-leak prevention: a PreToolUse hook that blocks a Write/Edit introducing a high-confidence secret (cloud keys, private-key blocks, provider tokens, assigned secret literals) before it reaches disk, plus a /secret-scanning:scan command to sweep a path or diff on demand. Fail-open, high-confidence-only, with a fixture escape.

plugins.name: payments
plugins.description: Payments and billing discipline (Stripe/Paddle/Braintree): PCI scope minimization via tokenization, integer-minor-unit money, signature-verified idempotent webhooks, subscription state machines and activation races, dunning and proration, and an append-only ledger with reconciliation — a firing payments skill and a /payments:review command. The failure class is double-charges and revenue leaks.

plugins.name: i18n
plugins.description: Internationalization and localization: semantic translation keys and catalog hygiene, ICU MessageFormat pluralization and gender, locale-aware dates/numbers/currency via Intl, RTL layout with logical properties, fallback chains, and tooling-driven extraction — a firing i18n skill and a /i18n:review command. For products shipping outside one language; skips a genuinely single-locale app.

plugins.name: llm-app
plugins.description: LLM application engineering: eval harnesses and regression gates (evaluate, don't eyeball), RAG pipelines (chunking, embeddings, retrieval quality + re-ranking, grounding and citation), prompt versioning, prompt-injection defense, token-cost control, and context management — a firing llm-app skill and a /llm-app:review command. Defers provider API specifics to the claude-api skill.

plugins.name: intent-guard
plugins.description: Lean, warn-only drift and corner-cutting guard for a coding agent. Anchors one declared intent X (with criteria) per session, injects a single continuous UserPromptSubmit reminder to review your diff before you call work done, and drives a drift-review diff-review-at-done against the session-base commit — an independent reviewer subagent when running top-level, honest self-review inside a subagent. No completion-blocking hook and no per-action ceremony; every output is advisory. Fills the mid-run intent-vs-action seam between coverage-check (entry) and work-verification (exit). Three warn-only hooks (UserPromptSubmit capture, PostToolUse note, Stop summary) plus the drift-review skill. Includes /intent-guard:intent and /intent-guard:status. Not tamper-proof and not a security boundary — cooperative and defeatable by an agent with shell access; it raises the visibility cost of casual drift, not an adversary.

plugins.name: ultra-deep-research
plugins.description: Deep-research harness that fans out parallel web searches, tiers sources by provenance, extracts date-stamped claims, and adversarially refutes them before synthesizing a cited report with a contradiction ledger and per-section confidence. Trigger 'ultra-deep-research' escalates to a Workflow-driven loop-until-dry fan-out with multi-vote refutation panels; standard depth runs a portable parallel-Agent fan-out. Ships /ultra-deep-research:research plus researcher and verifier subagents. Writes the report inline and to research/<topic>-<date>.md.

plugins.name: reuse-guard
plugins.description: Warn-only reuse-hygiene guard so a cooperating agent does not build on dead code. Two-tier by design. Tier 1 — language-agnostic ambient reuse-a-corpse: a PostToolUse hook greps incoming edits for references to symbols in a session-cached deprecated-symbol set (union of @deprecated / #[deprecated] / [Obsolete] / DEPRECATED-style markers) and warns once on a hit, silent otherwise. Tier 2 — on-demand /reuse-guard:check [symbol|path]: per-language dead-code tool shellout plus export-aware orphan / reachability detection and a full deprecated-reference report. Not tamper-proof and not a security boundary — a heuristic string match, warn-only, and defeatable by anyone with shell access; it raises the visibility of casual reuse-of-dead-code, not an adversary. Precision lives in the Tier-2 command, not the ambient hook.

plugins.name: compaction-advisor
plugins.description: Advice-only nudge to run /compact when a session grows long. A UserPromptSubmit hook counts user turns per session and, on a repeating 50-turn interval, prints one line suggesting /compact if an early chunk is no longer relevant to the current task — the model judges relevance from the nudge text. Resets on SessionStart source=compact|clear so it never nags right after a compaction or a context clear. Single self-healing state file per working tree, warn-only and fail-open: any error exits 0 silently, a corrupt or foreign-session state file re-seeds rather than going silent. No token API, no byte-size thresholds, and it never runs /compact itself — the user decides.

plugins.name: threejs
plugins.description: Three.js best practices: WebGPURenderer-first with automatic WebGL2 fallback (WebGPU is Baseline since Jan 2026), TSL shaders, react-three-fiber/drei integration, asset pipelines (glTF/Draco/KTX2), disposal and leak discipline, render-loop and draw-call performance, version-aware against the locked three revision. Includes /threejs:review.

plugins.name: fresh-take
plugins.description: Independent stronger-model second opinion at key moments — stuck debugging or an imminent irreversible action. /fresh-take:consult composes a facts-only brief (no leaning) and dispatches a blind stronger-model consultant with read-only repo access; it returns a Take, Risks, and one Alternative. A UserPromptSubmit reminder hook nudges the command on key-moment phrases. Advice only — it never blocks, gates, or edits anything.
